reason: Stale battle actions are accepted for 1.0 when they are rejected without mutating the current battle or duplicating rewards.
followUp: Keep the regression in the local release gate and revisit copy after the first public playtest.

reason: Return recap is useful for 1.0 even when the report has no immediate follow-up proxy in the same evidence window.
followUp: Add a stronger return follow-up signal after 1.0 telemetry review.

reason: A missing return recap signal is a manual release decision because local playtest can still prove the first-session route.
followUp: Run a live return smoke and add a clearer return evidence event.

reason: Post-session follow-up gaps are accepted only when the VK screens still show a concrete next action in manual playtest.
followUp: Tie post-session follow-up to a durable quest or exploration action after release.

reason: The next-goal signal may be absent in a narrow evidence window, but the release guide covers the player-facing route.
followUp: Add a report fixture for next-goal visibility after the first 1.0 pass.

reason: The local first-session route intentionally exercises the main tutorial path; alternate onboarding paths are accepted for 1.0 as manual coverage.
followUp: Add one automated alternate-path sample when onboarding branching becomes release-critical.

reason: School-specific runtime evidence covers all four schools through novice elite, sign equip, miniboss and rare seal; the first-session onboarding sample does not need to commit every school.
followUp: Extend first-session evidence after 1.0 if school choice distribution becomes a launch metric.
